title: Xander Lannick
content:
Xander Lannick (born in the late 1820s) is a former professional basketball player best known as head coach of the Cindermark Storks when they won the winter cup finals in 1853.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the winter cup finals, Xander Lannick remained with the Cindermark Storks for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Championship season.

The 1853 winter cup finals campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Xander Lannick averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Lunaris Memorial Arena in Lunaris.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Xander Lannick recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1853 playoffs.

Legacy.

Historians of the Cindermark Storks cite Xander Lannick's 1853 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Lunaris Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Playing style.

As head coach, Xander Lannick preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Cindermark Storks switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Personal life.

Xander Lannick retired to Lunaris and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1855.

Early life and college career.

Xander Lannick played college basketball in Lunaris before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as head coach.
